BulkTriageParams.description:
The scheduled-bulk request (slice 5, audit A-Mc): selector + patch, exactly the inline
bulk-triage shapes. The selector freezes to `target_ids` AT ENQUEUE (never a live selector
in a queue — D38/H8); the drain applies the frozen set.
BulkTriageParams.title: BulkTriageParams

DecisionEditRequest.description: The editable payload fields — anything set becomes the revoke+create pair's change set.
DecisionEditRequest.title: DecisionEditRequest

DecisionPayload.description: What a human decides — identity/lifecycle stamps are minted here, never client-supplied.
DecisionPayload.title: DecisionPayload

ExportParams.description:
The export lens — mirrors the M6 `SearchFilters` facets + the output format. Stored opaquely
as the report doc's `params`; the drain rebuilds a `SearchFilters` from it (a later slice).
ExportParams.title: ExportParams

SlaPolicy.description:
Per-canonical-severity SLA days + KEV override — editable via PUT /settings/sla.
D46 (#274): full-word knob names, HARD rename from crit_days/med_days (dev data disposable
— no aliases; the config doc reseeds on the next write).
SlaPolicy.title: SlaPolicy

UpdateView.description:
Partial update — unset fields keep their stored value. `owner`/`view_id` are not fields
here, so a rename-the-owner write is UNREPRESENTABLE (extra=forbid), not just forbidden.
UpdateView.title: UpdateView

ViewPreset.description:
The stored filter lens — MIRRORS `SearchFilters` one-to-one (drift fails the mirror
test). Closed vocabularies validated here; free-string fields keep the route caps.
ViewPreset.title: ViewPreset

get.description: Allowlist-shaped runtime facts for the read-only card — never a raw API passthrough.

schema.title: Response Get Opensearch Runtime Api V1 Admin Opensearch Runtime Get

get.description:
The seeded `system-roles` bundles (A-4/§13.6) — the UI renders whatever is seeded, so a
later 5th role appears without a client change. Read-only; bundle EDITING stays out of scope.

schema.title: Response List Roles Api V1 Admin Roles Get

get.description: The configured repo's snapshots, newest first (empty state until a repo ref exists).

schema.title: Response List Snapshots Api V1 Admin Snapshots Get

post.description:
Trigger an on-demand snapshot of the durability set (fire-and-forget; the list shows the
resulting state). 409 until a snapshot repository is configured.

schema.title: Response Take Manual Snapshot Api V1 Admin Snapshots Post

post.description:
Restore a snapshot into `restored-*` copies (never onto live indices — the drill's
rename semantics; promoting a copy is a deliberate manual step, so a mistaken restore
costs nothing).

schema.title: Response Restore Manual Snapshot Api V1 Admin Snapshots  Snapshot Name  Restore Post
Response List Tokens Api V1 Admin Tokens Get
Response Mint Api V1 Admin Tokens Post
Response Revoke Api V1 Admin Tokens  Token Id  Revoke Post

post.description:
Mint the sibling FIRST, then disable the old — the scanner is never token-less.
The sibling inherits the old token's expiry (rotation is not extension, task E m-7).

schema.title: Response Rotate Api V1 Admin Tokens  Token Id  Rotate Post
Response List Users Api V1 Admin Users Get
Response Create User Api V1 Admin Users Post
Response Set Disabled Api V1 Admin Users  Username  Disabled Patch
Response Password Reset Api V1 Admin Users  Username  Password Reset Post
Response Set Role Api V1 Admin Users  Username  Role Patch
Response Read Audit Log Api V1 Audit Get

get.description:
The prototype's Export CSV (M9d): streams the current audit lens — decorated,
injection-sanitized, constant-memory. Same inline bounds as the findings export: a cheap
pre-count 413s over `JAVV_EXPORT_MAX_ROWS` before any PIT opens; the PIT slot is the
principal's (429 at the cap) and is released when the stream ends.

Rail counts for the audit screen (M9d rework): entity_type/action/actor terms under the
same filters + D28 bound as the walk — the counts describe the current lens, server-side.
With `interval` (day|hour) the response adds `activity`: the audit lens's events-over-time
histogram across the picker window (quiet buckets included as zeros).

schema.title: Response Audit Facets Api V1 Audit Facets Get
Response List Clusters Api V1 Clusters Get
Response Rename Cluster Api V1 Clusters  Cluster Id  Name Put
Response Contributors Api V1 Contributors Get
Response List Decisions Api V1 Decisions Get
Response Create Api V1 Decisions Post

get.description:
M5d/FR-8: the risk-accept review surface for accept_final holders — ACTIVE risk-accept
decisions, soonest-expiring first (RULING, #30: creation is already SEC-2-gated, so this is
a review queue over standing acceptances, not a pending-approval workflow). Slice 4b
(operator re-ruling on the built 4a screen): the prototype rail's dims served server-side —
`q` (CVE contains) / `status` (derived from `expiry` at query time against `warn_days`,
mirroring the FE chip's window) / `created_by` / `scanner` (the column value, both|trivy|
grype) — plus facet counts under the same lens, one round trip.

schema.title: Response Approval List Api V1 Decisions Approvals Get
Response Edit Api V1 Decisions  Decision Id  Patch
Response Revoke Api V1 Decisions  Decision Id  Revoke Post

Response Facet Findings Api V1 Findings Facets Get
Response Group Findings Api V1 Findings Groups Get

get.description:
The Overview Top-components card: top packages by finding rows, each with a per-scanner
unique-CVE count. A now-only read (same rule as the severity-trend split — the card gates at
a rewound T); "all scanners" is the SUM of per-scanner uniques, never a cross-scanner
distinct count.

schema.title: Response Top Components Findings Api V1 Findings Top Components Get
Response Triage Api V1 Findings  Finding Key  Triage Patch
Response List Running Images Api V1 Images Get

One repo:tag's committed scan-event history for the DigestSubTimeline — build-change
(digest flips) and gap (per-scanner `scan_order` jumps) markers are derived client-side.

get.description:
Everything the panel renders in one read: the EFFECTIVE lifecycle + findings-cleanup
knobs for the cluster (override if set, else fleet default), whether each override doc
exists (the editor must know which doc it edits), the report TTL (fleet-wide), and the
non-secret snapshot repo ref (None until M2 config lands in the store).

get.description:
The EFFECTIVE timers for a cluster (its override if set, else the fleet default), plus
whether a per-cluster override exists — the editor needs to know which doc it is editing.

post.description:
First-login `must_change` exit + regular self-service change. Verifies the CURRENT password
(a hijacked cookie alone can't rotate credentials), then revokes every session of the user and
mints a fresh one — a concurrently-stolen session dies with the old password.

schema.title: Response Change Password Auth Password Post
